insight:
Yesterday was a solid training day with your 9.5km run completed at a steady aerobic pace - that 147 bpm average heart rate shows good zone 2 work. Your 15,980 steps and 3,108 calories burned demonstrate excellent activity levels, and that low resting heart rate of 43 bpm confirms your strong cardiovascular fitness is building nicely for Chester.

However, your elevated HRV at 114ms (above your 90-112ms baseline) combined with only 5.3 hours of sleep is sending mixed recovery signals. The higher HRV suggests your body handled yesterday's load well, but that sleep deficit could catch up with you. I'd recommend proceeding with today's planned session but listen to your body - if you feel sluggish, dial back the intensity slightly.

With 16 days until race day, prioritize sleep tonight. That short sleep likely disrupted your recovery hormones and appetite regulation. Focus on getting 7-8 hours tonight and ensure you're hitting that 175g protein target today to support muscle repair from yesterday's efforts.
